text:
Once upon a time by a quiet river, a little fox named Felix lived in a hollowed-out branch. He had little toys made of twigs and dried leaves, and he loved playing fetch with them at dawn. One day after a beautiful morning of playful frolicking, Felix saw something strange up ahead.

It was a big, clumsy owl who seemed lost. Felix didn't want anything to hurt the owl. Thinking quickly, he collected his tiny toys, made them into tiny lanterns by tying little bits of moss over them, and led the owl back to his safe haven under the trees.

In the forest clearing, Felix fed the owl berries that belonged to his friend bushes. The owl was quite grateful. Together they watched the sunrise over the waterfalls.

The owl told Felix stories of distant places, how the forest was different then.

Felix listened intently. "It sounds fascinating," he said.

As the days turned into weeks, the owl taught Felix about the songbirds that
